serp.title: Screen Size Calculator
serp.quality_notes: Ranked #1 in the observed SERP for 'monitor size calculator'. Snippet: enter any known screen dimension (cm/inches) and the others are calculated. Direct HTTPS fetch failed with a certificate mismatch (cert is for *.kasserver.com, a German shared-hosting provider), indicating a dated, cheaply hosted, poorly maintained site with misconfigured SSL.

serp.title: Screen Size Calculator - Omni Calculator
serp.quality_notes: Page on a large, entrenched branded calculator portal. Fetched: inputs are aspect ratio (preset/custom), diagonal, flat/curved toggle; outputs width, height, area, plus curved-screen depth. Authored content with named PhD authors and reviewers, substantial explanatory text. Notably lacks PPI in the main tool (only linked as a separate calculator).

serp.title: Projector Calculator | Screen Size Calculator | Aspect Ratio Calculator - ProjectorScreen.com
serp.quality_notes: Projector-screen retailer hosting a bundle of calculators (aspect ratio, screen size, distance). Oriented to projector buyers, not computer-monitor shoppers; commercial page, tangential intent match for 'monitor size calculator'.

serp.title: Multi-Monitor Calculator
serp.quality_notes: Exact-match-domain dedicated tool, but focused on planning multi-monitor layouts (drag/rearrange monitors, wallpaper preview) with PPI calculations. Niche angle rather than a simple diagonal-to-dimensions calculator; serves a different sub-intent.

serp.title: Free Screen Size Calculator - Optimize Your Viewing Experience
serp.quality_notes: Calculator page on an AV-integration software company's site (lead-gen adjacent). From snippet, a generic embedded screen size calculator with marketing copy; not a dedicated tool brand, limited depth discernible.

serp.title: Visual TV Size Comparison : Display Wars
serp.quality_notes: Fetched: visual side-by-side size comparison of two displays (size + aspect ratio inputs, graphical overlay output). Dated design with basic HTML forms and minimal styling; mobile responsiveness only recently added. No PPI, no area breakdown, minimal explanatory content.

serp.title: Screen size calculator - toolstud.io
serp.quality_notes: Fetched: one-developer toolbox site maintained since 2006 (footer shows v2.5.38, 2006-2024). Functionally the deepest: diagonal + resolution in, outputs width/height/area, aspect ratio, DPI, and four viewing-distance recommendations, plus FAQ. Utilitarian engineer-style design, desktop-oriented navigation, no comparison feature.

serp.title: Screen Size Calculator - See Thru Displays
serp.quality_notes: Calculator page on a transparent-display manufacturer's site. Snippet indicates diagonal + aspect ratio to dimensions with inch-to-mm conversion; a supporting utility page for the company's products, not a destination tool.

serp.title: Computer Laboratory - Projects: Display resolution calculator
serp.quality_notes: Academic page from University of Cambridge Computer Laboratory research project. High-authority .ac.uk domain but an old research-project page, not a polished consumer tool; ranks on authority rather than UX.

serp.title: Screen Size Calculator | Pro Display
serp.quality_notes: Calculator page on a UK display manufacturer's site; utility page supporting product sales. Limited depth discernible from snippet; commercial context rather than a dedicated tool brand.

competition_rationale: Torn between 3 (dedicated but weak/ugly incumbents) and 0 (entrenched branded incumbents), so per the conservative rule I score 2. Evidence for weakness: the observed #1 for the exact keyword, screen-size.info, is so poorly maintained that its HTTPS is broken (WebFetch returned a certificate mismatch — the cert is for *.kasserver.com shared hosting); Display Wars (position 6) has admittedly dated basic-HTML design, only recently added mobile support, and offers no PPI or area output; toolstud.io is a 2006-era single-developer toolbox with utilitarian design and no comparison feature; and at least four of the top-10 are retailer/manufacturer utility pages or thin company calculator pages (projectorscreen.com, xtenav.com, seethrudisplays.com, prodisplay.com) that only partially match monitor-buyer intent. No result in the top-10 fully combines diagonal+aspect-ratio inputs with width/height/area/PPI AND a size-comparison table on one modern page — the planned tool's exact feature set. Evidence against a higher score: Omni Calculator sits at position 2 — an entrenched, heavily branded portal with named PhD authors, reviewer credentials, curved-screen support, and deep explanatory content — and Inch Calculator (a second strong branded portal, clean UI, formulas, linked size charts) appears for the adjacent 'monitor dimensions calculator' query. There are no forums or gov pages in the top-10, so the score-5 condition clearly fails. Net: winnable SERP with visibly weak positions 1, 5, 6, 8, 10, but one to two genuinely strong branded incumbents cap the opportunity below a clean 3.

strengths: Entrenched branded portal with strong domain authority across thousands of calculators
Flat AND curved screen support (curved outputs include base depth and base width) - a differentiator most rivals lack
Named credentialed authors (PhD) and reviewers displayed, boosting E-E-A-T
Substantial explanatory content: aspect ratio theory, Pythagorean derivation, concrete iPhone X vs iPhone 8 Plus area comparison example
Preset plus custom aspect ratio input
weaknesses: No PPI output in the main tool - pixel density is exiled to a separate 'related calculator' page, forcing an extra click
No monitor size comparison table or visual comparison of two screens
Generic 'screen size' framing - not optimized specifically for monitor buyers (no common-monitor presets like 24/27/32 inch, no resolution presets)
No batch or multi-screen comparison capability
Portal template means the tool competes for attention with site chrome and cross-links to hundreds of unrelated calculators

strengths: Most complete output set observed: width/height/area in multiple units, aspect ratio, DPI/dpmm pixel density, and four viewing-distance recommendations
Large preset library (TVs, Apple devices, cinema screens, smartwatches)
Comprehensive FAQ explaining methodology (Pythagorean theorem, 30-degree field-of-view viewing distance rule)
weaknesses: Utilitarian, engineer-style single-developer design; dated look versus modern tool sites
Desktop-oriented navigation; mobile experience not clearly optimized
No side-by-side screen comparison feature at all
Requires pixel resolution input for full output - no simple diagonal+aspect-ratio-only quick path highlighted
Buried at /video/screensize.php on a generic toolbox domain with no monitor-specific content or size tables

strengths: Compelling visual overlay comparison of two screens drawn to scale - the stickiest feature in the niche
Simple two-input UX (size + aspect ratio) with inch/cm support including ultrawide 21:9
Practical example comparisons (e.g., 50 inch HD vs 65 inch 4K) aid comprehension
weaknesses: Dated interface: basic HTML forms, minimal styling; mobile responsiveness only recently added per its own update note
No PPI or pixel density output anywhere
No width/height/area numeric breakdown as a primary calculator output - it is comparison-only
No viewing distance guidance, no resolution handling
Minimal explanatory/educational content, limiting long-tail keyword coverage

strengths: Exact-match page title for 'monitor size calculator' keyword
Includes formula explanation, use cases, and an accuracy disclaimer
weaknesses: Template-like generic calculator page with minimal visual polish
Missing PPI, area, and aspect-ratio-input flexibility (takes resolution + diagonal only)
No comparison tables or common-monitor-size reference data
Brief, shallow explanatory content unlikely to satisfy informational intent
No mobile optimization signals observed
